title: Market Participants Survey—Fourth Quarter of 2025 - Bank of Canada
content:
## Market Participants Survey—Fourth Quarter of 2025
Results of the fourth-quarter 2025 survey conducted from December 16 to 30, 2025.
The Market Participants Survey results are based on questionnaire responses from about 27 financial market participants. You can view the latest quarterly data here.

### 1.1 What is your forecast for real GDP growth (year-over-year percentage change) in Canada?
GDP growth in Canada (year-over-year % change)
End of 2026 (n = 25)
End of 2027 (n = 23)
25th percentile of responses
75th percentile of responses
Note: Respondents were asked to calculate the four-quarter (year-over-year) growth rate based on the end of Q4. GDP is gross domestic product.

### 1.2 What is the probability of real GDP growth in Canada being in each of the following ranges at the end of 2026 and 2027?
GDP growth in Canada, average probability in each range (%)
End of 2026 (n = 24)
End of 2027 (n = 23)
Note: GDP is gross domestic product.

### 1.3 Please identify up to three upside risks to your growth outlook for Canada.
Share of respondents (%)* (n = 27)
Easing of trade tensions
Larger-than-expected fiscal stimulus
Stronger housing market
* Share of respondents who selected each risk

### 1.4 Please identify up to three downside risks to your growth outlook for Canada.
Share of respondents (%)* (n = 27)
Increase in trade tensions
Tightening of global financial conditions
Weaker consumer spending
* Share of respondents who selected each risk

### 1.5 How would you characterize the current level of Canadian GDP relative to potential output?
Characterization of output gap
Percentage of respondents (%) (n = 25)
Positive output gap (current GDP is greater than potential GDP)
Negative output gap (current GDP is less than potential GDP)
No output gap (current GDP is equal to potential GDP)
Note: GDP is gross domestic product. The output gap is the difference between the current level of Canadian GDP and the level of potential output.

### 1.6 What is the probability of the Canadian economy being in a recession in each of the following individual time frames?*
Probability of a recession in Canada (%)
In 0 to 6 months (n = 22)
In 6 to 12 months (n = 22)
In 12 to 18 months (n = 23)
In 18 to 24 months (n = 22)
25th percentile of responses
75th percentile of responses
* A recession is defined as two consecutive quarters of negative economic growth, as measured by real gross domestic product.

### 1.7 What is your forecast for annual total CPI inflation in Canada?
Headline CPI inflation in Canada (%)
End of 2026 (n = 26)
End of 2027 (n = 24)
5 years ahead (n = 23)
25th percentile of responses
75th percentile of responses
Note: Respondents were asked to estimate, based on the end of Q4, the four-quarter (year-over-year) consumer price index (CPI) inflation in Canada.

### 1.8 What is the probability of annual total CPI inflation in Canada being in each of the following ranges at the end of 2026 and 2027?
Annual CPI inflation in Canada, average probability in each range (%)
Range of CPI inflation
End of 2026 (n = 24)
End of 2027 (n = 22)
Note: CPI is the consumer price index.

### 2.1 What is your forecast for the Bank of Canada’s policy interest rate?
Policy interest rate (%)
25th percentile of responses
75th percentile of responses

### 2.2 How would you describe the balance of risks around your forecast for the Bank of Canada’s policy interest rate?
Percentage of respondents (%) (n = 26)
Skewed to a higher path
Skewed to a lower path
Risks are broadly balanced

### 2.3 What is your estimate of the long-term nominal neutral rate in Canada?
Nominal neutral rate (%) (n = 24)
25th percentile of responses
75th percentile of responses

### 3. According to your economic scenario, what is your point estimate for the following?
25th percentile of responses
75th percentile of responses
2-year Canadian bond yield
5-year Canadian bond yield
10-year Canadian bond yield
30-year Canadian bond yield
Oil price per barrel (West Texas Intermediate)
2-year Canadian bond yield
5-year Canadian bond yield
10-year Canadian bond yield
30-year Canadian bond yield
Oil price per barrel (West Texas Intermediate)
